mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar XK 5.0 V8 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned XK 5.0 V8 engine (AJ133) typically costs £4,000–£8,500 supply only depending on variant (naturally aspirated or supercharged). Customers have reported dealer quotes of around £15,000–£30,000 for engine replacement, while reconditioning or rebuilding is often in the £8,000–£10,000 range. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar XK 5.0 V8 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ133 is generally reliable, but it has well‑documented weaknesses. The water pump is the single most critical issue – it is a known failure point at 35,000–50,000 miles. Timing chain tensioner failure is also a well‑known vulnerability. Fuel injector issues and early fuel pump relay problems have also been reported. With regular maintenance and proactive replacement of known weak points, it can be a durable engine.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar XK 5.0 V8?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most common issues are water pump failure (coolant leaks, overheating risk), timing chain tensioner failure (rattling on startup, risk of catastrophic engine damage), fuel injector failure (rough idle, misfires), and fuel pump relay failure (early 2010 models, recall issued).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an XK 5.0 V8 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a tidy £8,000–£20,000 XK, a £4,000–£8,500 recon is 20‑50% of the car's value – often worth it on well‑maintained examples. If the timing chain has slipped and caused valve damage, a full engine replacement may be necessary. Get a full diagnostic assessment before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my XK's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The XK 5.0 V8 uses the AJ133 engine code. The naturally aspirated version produces 385 PS; the supercharged XKR version produces 510 PS. The engine code is stamped on the engine block and listed on your V5C. The badge on the boot identifies the variant – '5.0 V8' for the naturally aspirated version and 'XKR' for the supercharged version.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair an XK 5.0 V8 engine issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most issues – water pump failure, timing chain tensioner wear (if caught early), fuel injector failure, fuel pump relay – are repairable. However, if the timing chain has slipped, the engine is an interference design – pistons will strike valves. In this case, a full engine replacement is usually more cost‑effective than a rebuild.

replacementCosts.h2: Jaguar XK 5.0 V8 Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: Insurance jobs; warranty claims; full provenance

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the XK 5.0 pricing. Customers have reported dealer quotes of around £15,000–£30,000 for engine replacement, while reconditioning or rebuilding is often in the £8,000–£10,000 range. A rebuild of a 5.0 V8 engine can cost around £5,000 including parts and labour.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an XK 5.0 V8 engine typically takes 14‑20 hours at £75‑£110/hr (independent Jaguar specialist). The V8's size and the XK's packaging add significant labour complexity.

cta.label: → Compare XK 5.0 V8 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Water Pump Failure (The Defining Weakness)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, visible coolant leak from the front of the engine (around the water pump pulley area), sweet smell of antifreeze from the engine bay, "Coolant Low" warning message, overheating — there is no real temperature gauge on these cars, just an idiot light, and by the time it comes on it's almost too late.
problems.typicalMileage: 35,000–50,000 miles. Early 5.0L production cars are particularly susceptible.
problems.repairCost: £400–£800 [PLACEHOLDER] (water pump replacement — the intake manifold must be removed to access it).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; catastrophic overheating can cause engine failure).

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you smell antifreeze or see a coolant leak, stop driving immediately. Catastrophic engine damage can occur if the engine overheats.

problems.recommendation: The water pump is the single most critical weakness in the 5.0 V8 engine. It is a known failure point and should be considered a consumable item on these cars. The water pump has been revised multiple times with improved part numbers. One owner reported a water pump failure at 44,000 miles, and another at 33,000 km. There is no proper temperature gauge on these cars — just a warning light — so by the time you get a warning, it's often too late. Your nose is your best early warning system: if you smell antifreeze, investigate immediately. Replacing the water pump proactively at 40,000–50,000 miles is strongly recommended.

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure (AJ133)
problems.symptoms: Rattling, clanking, or ticking noise from the engine on startup, brief rattle from the timing chains on startup until oil pressure comes up and takes up the slack, engine warning light, loss of power, potential catastrophic failure if the chain slips.
problems.replacementCost: £4,000–£8,500 [PLACEHOLDER] (if chain slips and damages the engine — piston-to-valve contact is likely).

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling on startup, book an inspection immediately.

problems.recommendation: The AJ133 is known for timing chain tensioner issues. Early 5.0L models suffered from guide rails that were not strong enough — the metal part of the guide rails was made of aluminium which was weaker than the steel tensioner, causing the tensioner to dig into the guide and wear it down over time. JLR addressed this in 2015 with revised guide rails featuring a steel indent. There have been two interim upgrades before the latest version. The latest recommended upgrade parts are C2Z28431 and AJ813898. A Jaguar specialist noted that "the tensioner failure is such a well-documented failure point with the 5 litre V8". If the chain slips, piston-to-valve contact is likely. Change your oil frequently to prevent sludge build-up around the tensioner, which can restrict oil flow.

problems.title: Fuel Injector Failure (AJ133)
problems.symptoms: Poor running, poor fuel economy, hard starting, rough idle, misfires, engine detonation, engine warning light, rough idle and misfire that worsen over time.
problems.repairCost: £800–£2,000 [PLACEHOLDER] (injector replacement — recommended to replace all four injectors on the same cylinder bank if one is failing).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored injector issues can damage catalytic converters and the engine).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if misfire or rough running occurs.

problems.recommendation: Fuel injector issues are not uncommon on the direct-injection AJ133 engine. Direct injection engines require regular maintenance — dirty injectors can cause significant problems. Symptoms include rough idle, misfires, and hard starting. When replacing injectors, it's best to replace all four on the same bank due to the labour involved. Regular use of quality fuel and occasional fuel system cleaner can help prevent injector issues.

problems.title: Fuel Pump Relay Failure (Early Cars)
problems.symptoms: Engine stalling shortly after start-up, engine running rough, intermittent no-start, engine cuts out while driving.
problems.repairCost: £0 (recall — dealer repair free) / £100–£300 [PLACEHOLDER] (if out of warranty).

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if the engine cuts out while driving, this is a safety issue.

problems.recommendation: A recall was issued for 2010 XK and XF 5.0-litre models due to a faulty fuel pump relay that could cause the engine to stall shortly after start-up. Once restarted, the communication is regained and no further issues are said to result. However, if the engine cuts out while driving, pull over safely and have the vehicle recovered. Check your VIN against the recall database.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Jaguar XK 5.0 V8 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Essential maintenance — proactively replace at 40,000‑50,000 miles
Catch it early — cheaper than engine replacement
Full engine replacement — valve damage is almost certain
Worth fixing — common and well‑understood repair
Recall repair is free; out‑of‑warranty repair still cheap
Full engine replacement often more cost‑effective

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Jaguar XK 5.0 V8? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Jaguar service history with documented oil changes (every 10,000 miles or annually)
* Water pump replaced with the latest part number (AJ813909 or later)
* Timing chain tensioners upgraded to latest specification
* A 2011+ model (later cars had revised tensioners)
* Evidence of regular maintenance and care — these are premium engines that demand attention
avoidIf: * Any sign of coolant leaks or antifreeze smell — water pump warning
* Any sign of rattling on startup — timing chain tensioner warning
* No evidence of regular oil changes — the AJ133 is intolerant of neglect
* Poor service history — these are premium engines that demand strict maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Jaguar XK 5.0 V8 is one of the great modern grand tourers — a silky, powerful V8 in a beautifully styled package. The naturally aspirated 5.0 offers 385 PS and a glorious soundtrack; the supercharged XKR offers 510 PS and devastating performance. However, the AJ133 engine has well‑documented weaknesses — the water pump is the single most critical issue, and timing chain tensioner failure is a well‑known vulnerability. Buy on service history above all else — a vehicle with a replaced water pump, upgraded tensioners, and regular oil changes is a safe bet; a neglected example can be expensive to repair. As one owner put it, "It has been a very reliable car, the only issues I have had is with the water pump (a known issue that will need replacing)".

cta.label: → Compare XK 5.0 V8 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar XK 5.0 V8 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Water Pump — Has the water pump been replaced? When? With what part number? (AJ813909 is the latest recommended)
2. Coolant Leaks — Any smell of antifreeze? Any coolant loss? (water pump warning)
3. Cold Start — Any rattling for a few seconds on startup? (timing chain tensioner warning)
4. Service History — Regular oil changes (every 10,000 miles or annually) — this is non‑negotiable
5. Timing Chain History — Have the tensioners been upgraded to the latest parts? (C2Z28431 and AJ813898)
6. Recall Status — Early 2010 cars: Fuel pump relay recall completed?
7. Running Quality — Any rough idle, misfire, or hesitation? (injector warning)
8. MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures or any advisory notes
